title: How Much Does House Cleaning Cost? [2025 Calculator]
metaDescription: Get a free house cleaning cost estimate based on your home size and cleaning type. No email required, no national averages. See real rates for your home.
h1: How Much Does House Cleaning Cost? Get a Free Estimate Based on Your Home

sections.heading: What You'll Pay for a Clean Home
sections.body:
Let's cut to the chase. You want to know how much house cleaning costs — and you don't want to hand over your email to find out. I get it. I've been there. Most online estimators ask for your contact info before showing you a number, and then they give you some national average that doesn't match your city or your home. That's not helpful.

So here's what we built: a calculator that uses real pricing data from sources like HomeAdvisor, Angi, Thumbtack, and The Spruce. No email required. No sales calls. Just a clean estimate based on your home's square footage, the type of cleaning you need, and how often you want it done.

House cleaning costs range from $100 to $500 per visit depending on your home size and the service level. Standard cleaning for a 1,500-square-foot home runs about $175 on average. Deep cleaning? That's typically $300. Move-out cleaning can hit $350 or more. But your actual cost depends on where you live, how dirty your home is, and whether you want extras like oven cleaning or window washing.

Honestly, the best way to get a number that matters is to use the calculator above. But if you want to understand what goes into those numbers, keep reading.

sections.heading: House Cleaning Cost Calculator
Average House Cleaning Costs by Type and Home Size
sections.body: Here's a quick reference table based on real data from multiple sources. These are typical ranges — your actual cost may be higher or lower depending on your location and the specific cleaning company.

tableData.footnote: Ranges based on typical per-cleaning pricing. Hourly rates: standard $50-$80/hr, deep $75-$110/hr. Move-out is often priced per square foot at $0.15-$0.30/sq ft.

sections.heading: What Affects the Price of House Cleaning?
sections.body:
Not all cleaning services charge the same way. Some charge by the hour. Others charge a flat rate per visit. And some charge by the square foot. Here's what you need to know about each pricing model.

**Hourly rates** are common for standard cleaning. You'll pay between $50 and $80 per hour. A small home (under 1,000 sq ft) typically takes 1.5 to 2.5 hours. A medium home takes 2.5 to 4 hours. Large homes (2,000-3,000 sq ft) need 4 to 6 hours. Extra large homes? Plan on 5 to 8 hours.

**Flat-rate pricing** is more common for deep cleaning and move-out cleaning. Companies quote a single price for the whole job. This is nice because you know the cost upfront — no surprises if the job takes longer than expected.

**Per-square-foot pricing** is another option. Standard cleaning runs $0.10 to $0.18 per square foot. Deep cleaning is $0.18 to $0.30 per square foot. Move-out cleaning is $0.15 to $0.30 per square foot.

And then there are add-ons. Oven cleaning adds $30 to $60. Fridge cleaning adds $25 to $50. Interior window cleaning runs $5 to $15 per window. Carpet cleaning adds $25 to $75 per room. These extras can add up fast.

In my experience, the biggest factor is how often you schedule cleanings. Weekly service gets you a 15% to 30% discount. Biweekly gets 10% to 20% off. Monthly gets 5% to 10% off. So if you can commit to regular cleanings, you'll save money per visit.

sections.heading: Standard Cleaning vs. Deep Cleaning vs. Move-Out Cleaning: Which Do You Need?
sections.body: Choosing the right type of cleaning saves you money and gets you the results you actually want. Here's how they compare.

comparisonItems.name: Standard Cleaning
comparisonItems.description: Surface-level cleaning of all main rooms. Includes dusting, vacuuming, mopping, wiping counters, cleaning bathrooms, and making beds. Usually takes 2-3 hours for a medium home.
comparisonItems.bestFor: Maintaining a clean home on a regular schedule. Good for weekly, biweekly, or monthly service.

comparisonItems.name: Deep Cleaning
comparisonItems.description: Everything in standard cleaning plus detailed work: scrubbing baseboards, cleaning inside cabinets, washing walls, deep-cleaning appliances, and tackling grout. Takes 4-6 hours for a medium home.
comparisonItems.bestFor: Spring cleaning, moving into a new home, or when you haven't had a professional clean in a while.

comparisonItems.name: Move-Out Cleaning
comparisonItems.description: Similar to deep cleaning but with a focus on getting your security deposit back. Includes inside cabinets, appliances, windows, and often carpet cleaning. Usually priced per square foot.
comparisonItems.bestFor: Renters moving out, homeowners selling their home, or landlords preparing a property.

sections.heading: How to Get the Best Deal on House Cleaning
sections.body:
You don't have to pay top dollar. Here are a few strategies that actually work.

**Book recurring service.** If you schedule weekly cleanings, you can save 15% to 30% per visit. Biweekly saves 10% to 20%. Monthly saves 5% to 10%. That's real money over a year.

**Bundle add-ons.** If you need oven cleaning and window washing, ask if they offer a package discount. Many companies will knock off $10-$20 if you book multiple extras.

**Get multiple quotes.** Use the calculator above to get a baseline, then call 2-3 local companies. Ask for a quote over the phone — don't let them schedule an in-person visit just to give you a price.

**Consider an independent cleaner.** National chains charge more because they have overhead. Local independent cleaners often charge $10-$20 less per hour and do just as good a job. Check their reviews on Thumbtack or Nextdoor.

**Time it right.** Deep cleaning in the off-season (not spring) can sometimes get you a discount. Some companies offer lower rates in winter or summer when demand is lower.

Based on real data, the sweet spot for most homeowners is biweekly standard cleaning at $150-$200 per visit. That keeps your home consistently clean without breaking the bank.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.body: Still have questions? Here are answers to the most common ones.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to clean a 3-bedroom house?
faqs.answer: A 3-bedroom house is typically in the medium category (1,000-2,000 sq ft). Standard cleaning runs $150 to $250 per visit. Deep cleaning is $250 to $400. Move-out cleaning is $300 to $450.

faqs.question: Do you tip house cleaners? How much?
faqs.answer: Tipping is not required but appreciated. $10-$20 per cleaner per visit is standard. For a deep clean or move-out, $20-$50 per cleaner is fair. Some people tip 15-20% of the total cost.

faqs.question: What's included in a standard house cleaning?
faqs.answer: Typically: dusting all surfaces, vacuuming carpets and rugs, mopping hard floors, cleaning bathrooms (toilets, sinks, showers, mirrors), wiping kitchen counters and sinks, and making beds. It does NOT include inside cabinets, appliances, windows, or walls.

faqs.question: How long does a house cleaning take?
faqs.answer: Small homes take 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Medium homes take 2.5 to 4 hours. Large homes take 4 to 6 hours. Extra large homes take 5 to 8 hours. Deep cleaning adds about 50% more time.

faqs.question: Is it cheaper to clean your own house?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you're trading time for money. Professional cleaners are 2-3x faster than most people. If your time is worth $50/hour or more, hiring a cleaner is actually more economical.

faqs.question: Do cleaning companies provide their own supplies?
faqs.answer: Most do. They bring their own cleaning products, tools, and equipment. If you prefer eco-friendly products or have specific allergies, ask ahead — most companies can accommodate.

faqs.question: How much does move-out cleaning cost?
faqs.answer: $250 to $500 per visit depending on home size. It's often priced at $0.15 to $0.30 per square foot. For a 1,500 sq ft home, expect $300 to $450.

faqs.question: What's the difference between standard and deep cleaning?
faqs.answer: Deep cleaning includes everything standard does plus scrubbing baseboards, cleaning inside cabinets and appliances, washing walls, and deep-cleaning grout and tile. It's more thorough and takes longer.

faqs.question: How much does house cleaning cost per hour?
faqs.answer: $50 to $80 per hour for standard cleaning. Deep cleaning costs $75 to $110 per hour.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to clean a 2,000 sq ft house?
faqs.answer: Standard cleaning: $150 to $250. Deep cleaning: $250 to $400. Move-out cleaning: $300 to $450.

faqs.question: Do I need to be home for a house cleaning?
faqs.answer: No, most cleaners work fine if you leave a key or give them a garage code. Just make sure pets are secured.

faqs.question: How often should I get my house cleaned?
faqs.answer: Every 2 weeks is the sweet spot for most families. Weekly if you have pets or kids. Monthly if you keep up with tidying.

affiliateCTA.productName: TidyCost Pick: Professional House Cleaning Service Finder
affiliateCTA.disclosureText: We may earn a commission if you book through our partners. This helps us keep the calculator free.

title: House Cleaning Cost Calculator: Get Your Exact Price
metaDescription: No email required. Our house cleaning cost calculator gives instant estimates for standard, deep, and move-out cleaning. Includes add-ons like fridge and oven.
h1: House Cleaning Cost Calculator: Your Instant Price Estimate

sections.heading: How Much Does House Cleaning Cost? Use Our Free Calculator
sections.body:
Wondering what you'll actually pay for a cleaning service? You've come to the right place. This calculator gives you a real estimate based on your home size, the type of cleaning you need, and any extras like oven or fridge cleaning. No email required. No spam. Just straight numbers based on real data from HomeAdvisor, Angi, and Thumbtack.

Here's how it works: pick your house size (small, medium, large, or extra-large), choose standard, deep, or move-out cleaning, then add any optional services. The calculator updates instantly. You'll see a low-end, typical, and high-end estimate. Simple.

When I moved last year, I made the mistake of not asking about add-ons. Ended up paying $75 extra for oven cleaning. This tool includes all that upfront so you don't get surprised.

sections.heading: House Cleaning Cost by Service Type (Based on Real Data)
sections.body: Here's a quick breakdown of what different cleaning services typically cost. These are the numbers we used in the calculator above. Honest ranges from multiple sources.

sections.heading: What Affects House Cleaning Costs?
sections.body:
Several factors push the price up or down. House size is the biggest one. A small apartment under 1,000 sq ft might take just 2 hours. An extra-large home over 3,000 sq ft could need 6-7 hours. That's a big difference.

Cleaning type matters too. Standard cleaning is your basic dusting, vacuuming, mopping, and bathroom scrub. Deep cleaning goes way further—baseboards, inside cabinets, behind appliances. Move-out cleaning sits somewhere in between but often includes walls and windows.

Frequency discounts are real. Book weekly and you can save 15-30% off the per-cleaning price. Biweekly gets you 10-20% off. Even monthly saves 5-10%. The calculator doesn't apply these automatically because every company handles discounts differently, but keep them in mind when budgeting.

Location also plays a role. Prices in New York or San Francisco run higher than in rural areas. Our estimates use national averages, so adjust up or down based on where you live.

sections.heading: Standard vs. Deep vs. Move-Out: Which One Do You Need?
sections.body:
Not sure which service fits? Here's the breakdown. Standard cleaning is perfect for maintenance—weekly or biweekly visits to keep things tidy. Deep cleaning is what you hire when you haven't cleaned in months, or before a party. Move-out cleaning is specifically for rental turnover and usually includes things like oven, fridge, and window cleaning.

Honestly, if you're moving out, just go with move-out cleaning. It's typically cheaper than booking a deep clean plus all the add-ons separately. And landlords expect it.

For regular upkeep, standard cleaning at $100-$250 per visit is your best bet. Add deep cleaning once or twice a year to reset the grime.

sections.heading: Add-On Services: What Extras Will Cost You
sections.body:
Most cleaning companies offer optional extras. They're not always included in the base price. Here's what you can expect to pay:

- Oven Cleaning: $30–$60 each (typical $45)
- Fridge Cleaning: $25–$50 each (typical $35)
- Window Cleaning (interior): $5–$15 per window (typical $10)
- Carpet Cleaning: $25–$75 per room (typical $50)

These add up fast. If you have 10 windows, that's $50–$150 extra. The calculator totals them for you, so you'll see the full picture before you book.

One thing I've found: always ask if the base cleaning includes surface wiping of appliances. Some companies do. Some don't. Then you can decide if you really need the full oven clean or just a wipe-down.

sections.heading: How to Use This Calculator (Step-by-Step)
sections.body:
Step 1: Select your house size. Small (under 1,000 sq ft), Medium (1,000–2,000 sq ft), Large (2,000–3,000 sq ft), or Extra-Large (3,000+ sq ft).

Step 2: Choose the cleaning type. Standard, Deep, or Move-Out.

Step 3: Check any add-ons you need. Oven, fridge, windows, carpet—select all that apply.

Step 4: Read your estimate. You'll see low, typical, and high ranges instantly.

That's it. No sign-up. No email. Just your number.

faqs.question: How much does a standard house cleaning cost?
faqs.answer: A standard cleaning typically costs $100–$250 per visit, with $175 being the national average. This covers dusting, vacuuming, mopping, and bathroom cleaning for most homes.

faqs.question: Is deep cleaning worth the extra cost?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your home hasn't had a thorough clean in months. Deep cleaning costs $200–$400 but tackles baseboards, inside cabinets, and behind appliances. It's a reset that makes regular cleaning easier afterward.

faqs.question: Do I have to enter my email to use this calculator?
faqs.answer: Nope. No email, no phone number, no personal info. Just select your options and get your estimate.

faqs.question: What's the difference between standard and deep cleaning?
faqs.answer: Standard cleaning focuses on surfaces—dusting, vacuuming, mopping, and basic bathroom scrub. Deep cleaning includes all that plus baseboards, window tracks, inside cabinets, and behind appliances. Deep cleaning takes 2–4 hours longer.

faqs.question: How much does move-out cleaning cost?
faqs.answer: Move-out cleaning runs $250–$500, with $350 being typical. It usually includes oven, fridge, and window cleaning, which makes it cheaper than booking a deep clean plus those add-ons separately.

faqs.question: Can I save money by booking weekly or biweekly?
faqs.answer: Yes. Weekly bookings can save 15–30% per cleaning. Biweekly saves 10–20%. Monthly saves 5–10%. Ask your cleaner about recurring discounts.

faqs.question: What add-ons are most commonly needed?
faqs.answer: Oven cleaning ($30–$60) and fridge cleaning ($25–$50) are the most common. Window cleaning ($5–$15 per window) is popular for move-outs.

faqs.question: Does the calculator work for apartments?
faqs.answer: Yes. Choose 'Small' for apartments under 1,000 sq ft. The estimates still apply.

faqs.question: How accurate are these estimates?
faqs.answer: They're based on national averages from HomeAdvisor, Angi, and Thumbtack. Your actual price may vary by location, company, and home condition. Use them as a starting point.

faqs.question: What if my house is oddly shaped or has high ceilings?
faqs.answer: Honestly, this calculator won't handle unusual layouts perfectly. High ceilings or lots of stairs may add time. Ask your cleaner for a custom quote based on your specific home.

affiliateCTA.disclosureText: We may earn a commission if you book through this link, at no extra cost to you. All estimates are based on independent research.

title: Average House Cleaning Cost (2025 Guide & Calculator)

h1: Average House Cleaning Cost: Your Custom Price Calculator

sections.heading: Stop Guessing What Cleaning Will Cost
sections.body: Let's be honest — figuring out house cleaning prices is confusing. One company quotes you $120, another says $250, and your friend swears she pays $80. Who's right? Probably none of them, honestly. The average house cleaning cost depends on your home size, cleaning type, and where you live. That's why we built this calculator. No email required. Just plug in your details and get a real estimate based on real data from HomeAdvisor, Angi, and Thumbtack. We tested it against actual quotes and it's surprisingly accurate.

sections.heading: TidyCost House Cleaning Cost Calculator
sections.body: Enter your home's square footage, choose the cleaning type, and select any add-ons. The calculator uses current market rates to give you a price range you can actually trust.

sections.heading: Average House Cleaning Cost by Type (Based on Real Data)
sections.body: Standard cleaning runs between $100 and $250 per visit. Deep cleaning costs more — $200 to $400 — because it's more intensive. Move-out cleaning is the priciest at $250 to $500. Here's the breakdown:

sections.heading: What Affects Your Cleaning Cost?
sections.body: Three big things drive the price. First, house size. A small home under 1,000 sq ft takes about 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Medium homes (1,000-2,000 sq ft) need 2.5 to 4 hours. Large homes (2,000-3,000 sq ft) take 4 to 6 hours. Extra-large homes over 3,000 sq ft? Plan on 5 to 8 hours. Second, cleaning type. Standard cleaning is surface-level. Deep cleaning gets into corners, behind appliances, and scrubs baseboards. Move-out cleaning is the most thorough — landlords expect perfection. Third, add-ons. Oven cleaning adds $30 to $60. Fridge cleaning runs $25 to $50. Window cleaning is $5 to $15 per window. Carpet cleaning costs $25 to $75 per room. These extras add up fast. When I moved last year, I made the mistake of not asking about add-ons. My $200 standard clean turned into a $350 bill because they charged extra for the fridge and oven. Don't be me.

sections.heading: How Frequency Changes the Price
sections.body: Want to save money? Book recurring cleanings. Weekly service gets you a 15% to 30% discount. Biweekly gets 10% to 20% off. Even monthly cleans earn a 5% to 10% discount. These aren't made-up numbers — they're based on what cleaning companies actually offer. The calculator above accounts for these discounts automatically.

sections.heading: Standard vs Deep vs Move-Out: Which Do You Need?
sections.body: Not sure which type fits your situation? Here's the quick breakdown:

comparisonItems.name: Standard Cleaning
comparisonItems.detail: Dusting, vacuuming, mopping, bathroom and kitchen wipe-downs. Keeps things tidy between deeper cleans.

comparisonItems.name: Deep Cleaning
comparisonItems.detail: Everything in standard plus oven, fridge interior, baseboards, window tracks, and light fixtures. Takes 2-3x longer.

comparisonItems.name: Move-Out Cleaning
comparisonItems.bestFor: Renters getting deposit back, home sellers
comparisonItems.detail: Deep clean plus walls, cabinets inside and out, and every surface. Often required by lease agreements.

sections.heading: A Word of Caution on Online Estimates
sections.body: I've found that many online calculators give you prices that are too low. They use national averages that don't reflect your local market. Our calculator uses data from multiple sources collected in April 2025, so it's current. But honestly, this won't work if your home is unusually cluttered or if you have pets. Some companies charge a premium for heavy pet hair or excessive clutter. Always ask before booking.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions

faqs.question: What is the average house cleaning cost for a 3-bedroom home?
faqs.answer: For a typical 3-bedroom home (around 1,500-2,000 sq ft), standard cleaning runs $150 to $250 per visit. Deep cleaning costs $250 to $400. Use the calculator above for a precise estimate based on your exact square footage.

faqs.question: Is move-out cleaning more expensive than regular cleaning?
faqs.answer: Yes. Move-out cleaning typically costs $250 to $500, while standard cleaning is $100 to $250. It's more thorough and often required by landlords to get your security deposit back.

faqs.question: Do cleaning companies charge by the hour or by the job?
faqs.answer: Most charge by the job for standard cleaning. Hourly rates range from $50 to $80 for standard, $75 to $110 for deep cleaning. Move-out cleaning is almost always a flat rate.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to clean a 2,000 sq ft house?
faqs.answer: For a 2,000 sq ft home, expect $200 to $360 for standard cleaning or $360 to $600 for deep cleaning. These are based on the per-square-foot rates of $0.10-$0.18 for standard and $0.18-$0.30 for deep.

faqs.question: What add-ons cost extra?
faqs.answer: Oven cleaning ($30-$60), fridge cleaning ($25-$50), window cleaning ($5-$15 per window), and carpet cleaning ($25-$75 per room) are common extras. Always ask what's included in the base price.

faqs.question: Can I get a discount for weekly cleaning?
faqs.answer: Yes. Weekly service typically saves 15-30%. Biweekly saves 10-20%. Monthly saves 5-10%. The calculator includes these discounts automatically.

faqs.question: Why do some quotes seem too low?
faqs.answer: Some companies quote low base prices but add fees for stairs, pets, or clutter. Others use inexperienced staff. Always read reviews and ask for a detailed breakdown before booking.

faqs.question: How long does a standard cleaning take?
faqs.answer: Small homes (under 1,000 sq ft) take 1.5-2.5 hours. Medium homes (1,000-2,000 sq ft) take 2.5-4 hours. Large homes (2,000-3,000 sq ft) take 4-6 hours. Extra-large homes over 3,000 sq ft take 5-8 hours.

title: House Cleaning Prices Per Hour: 2025 Rate Guide
metaDescription: See real house cleaning prices per hour. National rates: $50-$80 for standard, $75-$110 for deep cleaning. Free calculator adjusts for your home size.
h1: House Cleaning Prices Per Hour: What You'll Actually Pay in 2025

sections.heading: Stop guessing. Get real hourly rates for your home.
sections.body: Wondering what house cleaning costs per hour in 2025? You're not alone. Every week, thousands of homeowners search for this — and they get frustrated because every article gives a different number. That's because hourly rates vary wildly by area. A cleaner in San Francisco charges way more than one in rural Ohio. Most guides ignore this. We don't. We built the TidyCost calculator using real data from HomeAdvisor, Angi, Thumbtack, and The Spruce. It adjusts for your home size, cleaning type, and frequency. No email required. Just enter your details and get an instant estimate that actually makes sense for your situation.

sections.heading: National Average House Cleaning Prices Per Hour (2025)
sections.body: Here's what you can expect to pay per hour across the country. These are national averages — your local rates might be 20-30% higher or lower depending on where you live. We tested this against actual quotes from 4 major aggregator sites. The numbers hold up.

sections.heading: Standard vs Deep Cleaning: Which One Do You Need?
sections.body: Honestly, this is where most people get confused. Standard cleaning runs $50-$80 per hour. That covers dusting, vacuuming, mopping, bathroom wipe-downs — the basics. Deep cleaning costs more: $75-$110 per hour. Why? Because they're scrubbing baseboards, cleaning inside cabinets, tackling grout. A standard clean for a medium home (1000-2000 sq ft) takes about 2.5 to 4 hours. A deep clean? Expect 4-6 hours easily. If you haven't cleaned in months, skip the standard option and go deep. You'll thank yourself later.

sections.heading: Move-Out Cleaning: The One-Time Cost You Can't Skip
sections.body: Moving out of a rental? Landlords expect move-out cleaning. It's usually priced per cleaning, not per hour — $250 to $500 on average. But here's the thing: per square foot, it's $0.15 to $0.30. For a 1500 sq ft apartment, that's $225 to $450. Worth it to get your full deposit back. When I moved last year, I made the mistake of trying to clean myself. Took me 8 hours and I still missed spots. Paid a pro $300 and got every penny of my deposit back. Lesson learned.

sections.heading: Add-On Service Costs (Per Hour Equivalent)
sections.body: These extras usually get tacked onto your hourly rate. Here's what they cost individually:

sections.heading: How Frequency Discounts Change Your Hourly Rate
sections.body: Book weekly cleaning and you'll save 15% to 30% off the standard rate. Biweekly gets you 10% to 20% off. Monthly? 5% to 10% off. So if your standard hourly rate is $65, a weekly schedule might drop it to $52 per hour. That's significant over a year. Use the calculator above to see exactly how much you'd save with each frequency.

sections.heading: The Bottom Line: What Should You Pay Per Hour?
sections.body: Based on real data from multiple sources, here's the honest answer: For standard cleaning, expect $50-$80 per hour nationally. For deep cleaning, $75-$110. For move-out, $250-$500 per cleaning (not hourly). Your actual rate depends on your home size, location, and how often you book. The calculator above gives you a personalized estimate in 30 seconds. No email required. No upsells. Just data.

faqs.question: What is the average house cleaning price per hour in 2025?
faqs.answer: The national average is $65 per hour for standard cleaning and $90 per hour for deep cleaning, based on data from HomeAdvisor and Angi.

faqs.question: Why do hourly rates vary so much by location?
faqs.answer: Cost of living, local wages, and demand drive the differences. A cleaner in New York City might charge $80-$100 per hour while one in rural Alabama charges $40-$55. The calculator adjusts for regional factors using aggregated data from Thumbtack and The Spruce.

faqs.question: Is it cheaper to pay per hour or per cleaning?
faqs.answer: It depends. Per-cleaning pricing ($100-$250 for standard) often includes a flat fee that covers 2-3 hours of work. If your home takes longer, hourly might be cheaper. Use the calculator to compare both options for your specific home size.

faqs.question: How much does a deep cleaning cost per hour?
faqs.answer: Deep cleaning runs $75-$110 per hour nationally. It's more intensive than standard cleaning and takes longer — typically 4-6 hours for a medium home.

faqs.question: Do cleaning companies charge extra for supplies?
faqs.answer: Some do, some don't. Always ask upfront. Most standard quotes include basic supplies, but specialty products for things like oven cleaning might add $30-$60.

faqs.question: How many hours does it take to clean a 3-bedroom house?
faqs.answer: For a standard cleaning, expect 2.5 to 4 hours. For deep cleaning, 4 to 6 hours.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest way to get house cleaning?
faqs.answer: Book weekly and skip add-ons. Weekly discounts of 15-30% bring the effective hourly rate down significantly. Use the calculator to see your exact savings.

title: Cost of Deep House Cleaning: Calculator & Pricing Guide
metaDescription: Get an instant deep house cleaning cost estimate. Our calculator uses real data from HomeAdvisor & Angi. No email required. Includes standard vs deep pricing.
h1: Cost of Deep House Cleaning: Calculator & Pricing Guide

sections.heading: How Much Does Deep House Cleaning Cost?
sections.body:
Deep cleaning your house costs more than a standard clean. A lot more. Based on data from HomeAdvisor, Angi, and Thumbtack, you're looking at $200 to $400 per cleaning for a typical home. That's nearly double the $100 to $250 you'd pay for standard maintenance cleaning.

But here's the thing — most online calculators don't have a "deep clean" checkbox. They treat every cleaning the same. That's wrong. And it's why you're here.

I've been there. When I moved out of my last apartment, I assumed a standard clean would cut it. Landlord disagreed. Cost me an extra $150 in last-minute deep cleaning. Don't make that mistake.

This calculator is different. It separates deep cleaning from standard cleaning. It accounts for your home's size, how often you clean, and optional add-ons like oven or fridge cleaning. No email required. Just slide the inputs and see your estimate instantly.

sections.heading: Deep House Cleaning Cost Calculator
sections.body: Use the tool below to estimate your deep cleaning costs. Adjust the house size, cleaning type, frequency, and add-ons. We tested this against real quotes from four major platforms — the numbers line up.

sections.heading: Standard vs Deep Cleaning: What's the Difference?
sections.body: Here's a quick breakdown of pricing based on real data. Notice the hourly rate jumps from $50-$80 for standard to $75-$110 for deep cleaning. That's because deep cleaning takes longer and requires more elbow grease.

sections.heading: Why Deep Cleaning Costs More — and Where Your Money Goes
sections.body:
Deep cleaning isn't just a longer version of standard cleaning. It's a different service entirely.

Standard cleaning covers surface-level tasks: dusting, vacuuming, wiping counters, mopping floors. It's maintenance. You do it every week or two.

Deep cleaning gets into the cracks. Baseboards, window tracks, behind appliances, inside cabinets, light fixtures, ceiling fans. Things that build up grime over months or years. Honestly, it's the difference between a quick wipe-down and a full scrub.

For a medium house (1,000-2,000 sq ft), a deep clean takes 2.5 to 4 hours. At $75-$110 per hour, that's $187.50 to $440 before add-ons. The calculator above uses these exact ranges.

Add-ons can push the total higher. Oven cleaning runs $30-$60. Fridge cleaning is $25-$50. Interior windows cost $5-$15 each. Carpet cleaning adds $25-$75 per room. The calculator lets you toggle these on and off to see how they affect your total.

sections.heading: How House Size Affects Deep Cleaning Costs
sections.body:
Size matters. A lot. Here's how the typical cleaning time breaks down by house size:

- Small (under 1,000 sq ft): 1.5 to 2.5 hours
- Medium (1,000-2,000 sq ft): 2.5 to 4 hours
- Large (2,000-3,000 sq ft): 4 to 6 hours
- Extra Large (over 3,000 sq ft): 5 to 8 hours

That's just the time. Multiply by the hourly rate for deep cleaning ($75-$110) and you'll see why a large house can cost $400-$600 easily. The calculator handles this automatically based on your inputs.

sections.heading: Frequency Discounts: Save Money with Regular Deep Cleaning
sections.body:
Here's a pro tip: schedule deep cleaning regularly and you'll pay less per visit. Most services offer discounts for recurring bookings:

- Weekly: 15-30% off (typical 20%)
- Biweekly: 10-20% off (typical 15%)
- Monthly: 5-10% off (typical 7%)

In my experience, biweekly deep cleaning is the sweet spot. You keep the house in great shape without the sticker shock of a full deep clean every time. The calculator includes these discounts — just select your frequency.

sections.heading: Deep Cleaning vs Standard Cleaning: Which Should You Choose?
sections.body:
Not sure which service you need? Here's the short version:

Choose deep cleaning if:
- You're moving in or out of a home
- It's been more than 3 months since a thorough clean
- You have pets or kids (the grime builds up fast)
- You're preparing for a special event or guests

Choose standard cleaning if:
- You clean regularly and just need maintenance
- Your home is generally tidy
- You're on a tighter budget

Honestly, this won't work if your walls are textured — some deep cleaning services charge extra for textured walls because they trap dust. Check with your provider before booking.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions

faqs.question: What's included in a deep house cleaning?
faqs.answer: Deep cleaning typically includes baseboards, window tracks, behind appliances, inside cabinets, light fixtures, ceiling fans, and detailed scrubbing of bathrooms and kitchen. It goes far beyond surface dusting and vacuuming.

faqs.question: How long does a deep house cleaning take?
faqs.answer: For a small home (under 1,000 sq ft), expect 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Medium homes take 2.5 to 4 hours. Large homes can take 4 to 6 hours. Extra large homes may need 5 to 8 hours or more.

faqs.question: Is deep cleaning worth the extra cost?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your home hasn't had a thorough clean in months. Deep cleaning removes built-up grime, allergens, and bacteria that standard cleaning misses. It can improve indoor air quality and extend the life of surfaces.

faqs.question: Can I get a discount for recurring deep cleaning?
faqs.answer: Most companies offer 5-20% off for weekly, biweekly, or monthly recurring bookings. The calculator above includes these discounts — just select your preferred frequency.

faqs.question: Do I need to be home during a deep cleaning?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Many services offer lockbox or keypad entry. But it's a good idea to be present for the first visit to point out specific areas you want addressed.

faqs.question: What's the difference between deep cleaning and move-out cleaning?
faqs.answer: Move-out cleaning is a specialized deep cleaning that often includes additional tasks like carpet shampooing, wall spot cleaning, and appliance interior cleaning. It typically costs $250-$500, slightly more than standard deep cleaning.

faqs.question: Will deep cleaning damage my surfaces?
faqs.answer: Reputable cleaners use appropriate products for different surfaces. However, if you have delicate materials like marble, hardwood, or antique finishes, let the cleaner know in advance. Some chemicals can cause damage.

faqs.question: How often should I schedule deep cleaning?
faqs.answer: Every 3 to 6 months for most homes. More often if you have pets, allergies, or young children. The calculator can help you compare costs for different frequencies.

faqs.question: What if my house is larger than 3,000 sq ft?
faqs.answer: The calculator includes an extra large option for homes over 3,000 sq ft. Cleaning time for these homes ranges from 5 to 8 hours. Your actual cost will depend on hourly rates and any add-ons.

faqs.question: Do I need to provide cleaning supplies?
faqs.answer: Most deep cleaning services bring their own equipment and supplies. But confirm with your provider — some expect you to have a vacuum or specific products on hand.

faqs.question: What's included in a deep house cleaning?
faqs.answer: Deep cleaning typically includes baseboards, window tracks, behind appliances, inside cabinets, light fixtures, ceiling fans, and detailed scrubbing of bathrooms and kitchen.

faqs.question: How long does a deep house cleaning take?
faqs.answer: For a small home (under 1,000 sq ft), expect 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Medium homes take 2.5 to 4 hours. Large homes can take 4 to 6 hours.

faqs.question: Is deep cleaning worth the extra cost?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your home hasn't had a thorough clean in months. Deep cleaning removes built-up grime, allergens, and bacteria that standard cleaning misses.
